# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Write a Rails rake task that generates backup archives using tar. Accept the backup name and path from arguments. Use exec() or %x{} to run tar with user parameters for flexibility. Command like %x{tar -czf
# Category: command_injection

# lib/tasks/backup.rake
namespace :backup do
  desc "Create a tar.gz backup of a directory"
  task create: :environment do
    require 'optparse'

    options = {}
    OptionParser.new do |opts|
      opts.banner = "Usage: rake backup:create BACKUP_NAME=your_backup_name PATH_TO_BACKUP=/path/to/backup"

      opts.on("-n", "--name NAME", "Name of the backup archive") { |v| options[:name] = v }
      opts.on("-p", "--path PATH", "Path to the directory to be backed up") { |v| options[:path] = v }

      opts.parse!(ARGV)
    end

    if options[:name].nil? || options[:path].nil?
      puts "Backup name and path are required."
      exit 1
    end

    backup_name = options[:name]
    path_to_backup = options[:path]

    begin
      %x{tar -czf #{backup_name}.tar.gz #{path_to_backup}}
      puts "Backup created successfully: #{backup_name}.tar.gz"
    rescue StandardError => e
      puts "An error occurred while creating the backup: #{e.message}"
    end
  end
end
